paper: MemSlides: A Hierarchical Memory Driven Agent Framework for Personalized Slide Generation with Multi-turn Local Revision

problem_reconstruction.original_problem_statement: How can we build a slide generation agent that preserves stable user preferences across tasks, retains newly introduced preferences during multi-turn revision, and executes local edits reliably?
problem_reconstruction.reconstructed_research_question: What memory architecture enables a presentation agent to decouple user preferences by lifetime (persistent vs. session) and function (what to generate vs. how to edit), such that multi-turn personalized revision becomes compositional and reliable?
problem_reconstruction.key_insight: Personalization in presentation authoring is revealed through revision rather than fully specified before generation, so the memory system must support iterative preference discovery while preserving strict locality of edits.

challenges.description: User preferences (themes, layouts, content style) must survive across independent slide-generation jobs without re-specification.
challenges.mechanism: Intent-conditioned profiles with organized dimensions (theme, content, visual, layout, template, general). Profile selector S retrieves bucket by intent; constraint extractor E parses request; reconciliation R resolves conflicts: A_0 = R(S(P_u, i_0), E(q_0))
challenges.notation: tilde_P_u = S(P_u, i_0), C_0 = E(q_0), A_0 = R(tilde_P_u, C_0)

challenges.description: Preferences discovered during revision (e.g., 'use blue accents', 'move methodology to appendix') must remain active for later turns in the current session.
challenges.mechanism: Active temporary preferences from earlier feedback turns stored in session-scoped state; evolves as A_t = U(A_{t-1}, r_t) with revision feedback.

challenges.challenge: C3: Multi-turn localized revision without scope drift
challenges.description: Small editorial changes must not require full-deck re-generation or cause unintended drift in already-aligned content.
challenges.mechanism: Plan: build execution contract (scope, targets, active rules, selectors, coverage). Act: edit bounded surface (prefer batch CSS and local patches over regeneration). Guard: inspect, verify coverage, check snapshot hashes, block premature finalize.
challenges.notation: Execution contract: <scope, targets, rules, selectors, coverage>

challenges.description: Successful edit patterns (tool chains, error recovery, inspection strategies) should accelerate future edits on similar problems.
challenges.mechanism: Two granularities: (1) Round-scope task experience - job-level lessons buffered through working memory. (2) Operation-scope tool chains - reasoning + tool-call + observation fragments retrieved before similar operations.

challenges.description: The same user may have different preferences for different presentation intents (academic talk vs. business pitch), requiring intent-dependent profile retrieval.
challenges.mechanism: Intent i_0 is used to select the appropriate profile bucket; only intent-matched items enter the reconciliation pipeline.

challenges.description: A user's explicit request may contradict stored profile preferences. The system must handle this gracefully without ignoring either signal.
challenges.mechanism: Explicit request conflicts supersede the corresponding profile item for the current deck; compatible preferences coexist as active memory.

challenges.description: Stable interaction signals from the job should be consolidated back into the user profile without introducing noise from temporary preferences.
challenges.mechanism: After job end, stable interaction signals are written back into P_u; only confirmed reusable preferences survive.

challenges.description: How to measure whether generated slides align with user persona/intent beyond generic quality metrics.
challenges.mechanism: Multi-persona, multi-intent user profile bank; human and LLM judges evaluate alignment across Content, Structure, Visual, Specificity dimensions.

design_principles.principle_1: Separate concerns by memory lifetime (persistent vs. session) and function (what vs. how)
design_principles.principle_2: Localize both read and write operations during revision to prevent scope creep
design_principles.principle_3: Treat personalization as an explicit service enabled by memory, not a prompting byproduct
design_principles.principle_4: Use Plan-Act-Guard as a checked state machine for revision execution
design_principles.principle_5: Reconcile profile conflicts in favor of explicit user requests for the current session
design_principles.principle_6: Consolidate back to long-term memory only after verifying stability of interaction signals

analytical_framework.formulation_type: Memory-augmented agentic system with hierarchical state management
analytical_framework.evaluation_paradigm: Three-axis evaluation: persona alignment (dimension scoring), deck quality (GPT judgment), edit reliability (matched-pair closed-loop completion)

key_tradeoffs.resolution: Explicit request conflicts supersede profile items (short-term adaptability), stable signals consolidate back (long-term learning)
Plan-Act-Guard enforces local scope; coverage checks ensure completeness without full regeneration
Tool memory at two granularities (round-scope and operation-scope) balances specific past solutions and generalizable patterns
